destinations.evidence: Incident recorder keeps a bounded VecDeque and drops the oldest entry before appending newest.
Reader returns incidents newest-first and caps by configured capacity.
Writer is best-effort under contention, preserving payload builder latency.

equivalence_argument: Exact mapping in ritual-metrics scheduled incident ring buffer.
rationale: The only in-cluster candidate directly defines a current bounded diagnostics retention invariant. The bounded neighbors describe scheduler transaction and service mechanics but do not justify a direct historical lineage edge for this metrics buffer.
statement: record_scheduled_incident must append enabled scheduled incidents to an in-memory bounded ring buffer with monotonic sequence numbers, evicting the oldest retained incident when capacity is full, and recent_scheduled_incidents(limit) must return cloned traces newest-first up to the lesser of the requested limit and configured capacity.
suggested_falsification_tests: Configure scheduled incident capacity to 2, record three enabled incidents, and assert exactly two remain, the first recorded incident was evicted, retained sequence numbers are monotonic, and recent_scheduled_incidents returns the newest incident first.
Record incidents with retention disabled or limit zero, as supported by the metrics configuration/API, and assert retrieval does not exceed the configured enabled capacity semantics.
summary: Scheduled incident traces are retained newest-first in a bounded best-effort buffer.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Set capacity=2, record incidents A/B/C, then query limit=10 and assert C,B returned, A evicted; force try_lock contention and assert drop counter increments.
title: Scheduled incident traces are retained newest-first
